senses.gloss: to give [with لِ (li) ‘someone’] a veiled hint of
to be intelligent, to have a good grasp
to chant, to intone; to psalmodize
to accuse of mispronouncing or employing barbarisms in speaking
tone or mode of speech, idiom, the characteristic way any animal or group sounds like

examples.text: فَلَعَرَفْتَهُم بِسِيمَاهُمْ وَلَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِي لَحْنِ ٱلْقَوْلِ
examples.english: Thou shouldst know them by their marks and thou shalt know them by the manner of their talk.

examples.text: وقد وقعت لحون في قراءة القرآن الكريم في عهد الصحابة رضوان الله عليهم ، فاستنكروها، لأنها قد أفسدت المعني

senses.gloss: intelligence, quickwittedness, nimbleness, verbal noun of لَحِنَ (laḥina)
